item.content:encoded:
What do I see in this object? An anomaly and a fairly predictable joke. Yes, the joke is not bad and the design is chosen perfectly, but it is still just a joke and there is nothing behind it. I agree with some of the commenters above, it could very easily be a -J, but this is in the main series.
If you compare this to the very similar in my opinion SCP-3078, you may understand the reason for my disappointment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
YOUR HEART IS NOW BEATING MANUALLY

that actually gave me a tiny heart attack +1

item.title: Re: Downvote
item.content:encoded: Talking down to the author is a very fast way to make your commentary, however valid it may be, invalid in the eyes of the crestor. No one likes to engage in positive collaboration with someone they feel doesn't respect them.

i see the plan here, now im breathing manually, my toes are uncomfortable, im aware of my inevitable doom, AND IM AWARE OF MY PERIPHERAL VISION, NOOOOOOOOOOOO!

seriously i like this +1

If I open SCP-001, I'll find the very same concept behind this article in the first messages of that page. This isn't a unique concept, it's just a memetic kill agent. So, when an article takes an old concept to build around it, I expect that content to be improved or even modified and twisted as the story progresses so the article can have something unique and to differentiate it from others. Unfortunately, that is not the case here.

I have several problems with this article; the fact the memetic kill agent wasn't censored at all, the fact there is no narrative and it exists solely for the punchline, the fact the very own creator of the message didn't die, despite it being a memetic kill agent…

If this was a -J, I really wouldn't care that much, but this is in the main series, so I have to look at it with scrutiny. I'm sorry, but this is a downvote from me.

I'm sure you will come to make better articles in the future, friend. I'd recommend reading series 3 and 4 for inspiration! Good luck in your future writing endeavors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, but I think the problem with this lies in the fact that it sacrifices in-universe logic for it to work, an example of this being the memetic message being uncensored. It's not an article that stands on its own and happens to be funny, it's an article tailored around a specific punchline, it could very easily be a -J.

item.title: Downvote
item.content:encoded:
Well, this is just sad. For me, this article fails because it tries to be a joke. Someone previously said that SCPs can be funny, but we need to establish a limit here. When an article has a joke in it, that's fine, but when your whole article tries to be a joke, then it can only be a -J.

It's a random message that kills people, and the message is still in the article for everyone to read anyway? In universe it should be censored, but that would kill the punchline, and here we have the problem again: the whole article is a joke, so without the punchline it becomes pointless. Come on, at least create a narrative to keep the reader engaged until the secret killer funny message is revealed or anything like that. There is no story, no interesting anomaly, there is nothing good here, really.

I think we all need to remember that joke SCPs are separate from the main list for a reason. - 1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is dumb, which might be okay if it were actually funny, but it isn’t. It might be slightly humorous if the procedures and description had enough buildup before the chat log that the dumb joke was an unexpected punch line, but the first parts of the file were so sparse that I didn’t even have a clear expectation of what it was before I got to the log, at which point it became immediately obvious what the punchline was going to be.

There should be a collapsible or inoculation or something, since apparently we’re just reading the naked cognitohazard unprotected. That would make your article make more sense in universe and possibly build a little bit of suspense to make the punchline end better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the funniest new skips I've read in a while. Jesus fuck. Holy shit. Please take my Upvotes. All +9001 of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing style and use of image/formatting is strong, but the joke doesn't land for me. As humour's so subjective, it's a no-vote from me, but I look forward to seeing what your second article is!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In universe, there should probably be some sort of protection/warning on the page for the cognitohazard. Idk, seeing the phrase directly in the chatlog took me out of immersion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why exactly should it be a -J? Mainlisters can be funny articles too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I think this should be a SCP-J, it seems so out of place for some reasons. Nevertheless, I found it's quite basic yet effective

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but if it counts as a cognitohazard, wouldn’t it need some censorship or inoculation so a researcher doesn’t fall prey to it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
there's some pretty good scps that could just as easily been extranromal events, but were made into scp's. i dont see the harm in it as long as it tells a satisfying story or is unique enough to stand on its own.

3128 and 5320 come to mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it's just a one time event it's not an SCP though. But i think the description is just incorrect since the confinement procedures seems to imply it's not a one case scenario but rather a meme that causes heart attack. And i guess the description is vague and misleading to preserve the twist of the chatlog and that's where lies the problem of this SCP.

Don't mistake me, it's funny. But everything is just an excuse for the chatlog story, who is the real story and good format. It works without trying to force it as an SCP. For example, SCP-3078 does a better job at being an SCP article with the same premise. This would work better as a short tale or a extranormal event like previously said.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's funny but there is a page for Extranormal Events.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm surprised this hasn't been done before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This didn't really get to me. But since it does everything right, I'll still upvote.

Nice first SCP. I had a big dumb smile on my face by the end.

Easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In all honesty, I didn't find this funny in the slightest.
Sorry, but I'm gonna have to give this a -1.
I'm now upvoting manually, I guess…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think my heart just stopped beating

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember I saw something on some website before that was like this. I like this, great use of the discord css.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, now I'm breathing manually
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congrats on the first article. Fun read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not really my type of thing; I didn’t find this funny. -1

item.title: SCP-5922
item.content:encoded:
Oh hey it's my first SCP!
Massive thanks to aismallard for all the feedback and help writing this!
And also big thanks to everyone who gave feedback:
Yossipossi
Rounderhouse
Alces_alces
Ralliston
Deadly Bread

Image sources:
beans.png: Made by me, releasing under CC-BY-SA 3.0
prosp.png: Made by me, releasing under CC-BY-SA 3.0
yisso.png: Made by me, releasing under CC-BY-SA 3.0
tohos.png: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Crocs_Warning_Notice_ewenm.png
nico.jpg: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:PUPPET.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it small stupid and fun +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very simple, yet appealing. +1
